target.title: fix(agents): guard message provider tool name reads
target.body:
## Summary
- Guard `filterToolsByMessageProvider()` against tool descriptors whose `name` getter throws while provider allow/deny policy is applied.
- Avoid reading descriptor names when no message-provider policy exists, preserving the original tool list for providers such as `guildchat`.
- Preserve current-main normalized provider coverage while adding focused regression coverage for unreadable names in the voice-policy path and the no-policy path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Ongoing tool/schema fuzzing hardening work.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Message-provider tool policy filtering no longer crashes when a malformed tool descriptor has an unreadable `name` getter; provider policy filtering omits that malformed descriptor and keeps healthy allowed siblings.
- Real environment tested: Local linked OpenClaw worktree rebased onto current `origin/main` (`f60943717ef57412872233a45f2b61e4f95f4c2a`), branch `fuzz-tool-schema-next75-20260603`, commit `2170cb49380768dace3cd0fa2dde3670a82bd44f`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-next75-rebased2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `git push --force-with-lease origin fuzz-tool-schema-next75-20260603`.
- Evidence after fix: The focused Vitest file passed 1 file / 9 tests; scoped `oxfmt --check` passed; scoped `run-oxlint.mjs` passed; whitespace diff check passed; branch autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` with `overall: patch is correct (0.87)`.
- Observed result after fix: The synthetic unreadable-name descriptor is dropped only when a provider policy must inspect names, `tts` is still denied for normalized voice providers, and providers without a policy return the original tool descriptors without reading names.
- What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:changed` did not complete because maintainer remote runners are not authenticated in this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `blacksmith testbox list --all` failed with `not authenticated`; AWS Crabbox reported provider `aws` but failed coordinator `/v1/runs` and `/v1/leases` with HTTP 401 `unauthorized`; delegated `blacksmith-testbox` Crabbox failed at `blacksmith testbox warmup` with `not authenticated`.
- Before evidence: The focused repro added in this PR failed before the fix with `fuzzed unreadable tool name` when the message-provider policy path read the tool `name` getter.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git fetch origin main fuzz-tool-schema-next75-20260603 --prune`
- `git rebase origin/main` (conflict resolved in `src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts` to combine current-main normalized provider tests with guarded descriptor reads)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-next75-rebased2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.ts src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `blacksmith testbox list --all` (blocked: not authenticated)
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label fuzz-tool-schema-next75-check --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (blocked: coordinator HTTP 401 unauthorized)
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --label fuzz-tool-schema-next75-check --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"` (blocked: Blacksmith not authenticated)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added coverage that `filterToolsByMessageProvider()` skips unreadable tool-name descriptors when an active provider policy must inspect names.
- Added coverage that the no-policy path returns the original descriptors without reading their `name` fields.
- Preserved current-main normalized voice-provider coverage.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The new focused repro failed before the fix with `fuzzed unreadable tool name`.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed tool descriptors are omitted from provider-policy-filtered tool lists instead of crashing the turn before model content.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

A malformed descriptor with an unreadable name is now dropped when a message-provider allow/deny policy applies.

How is that risk mitigated?

The no-policy fast path still returns original descriptors without reading names, healthy sibling tools are preserved, duplicate readable names are still counted, and provider allow/deny semantics are unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and normal PR CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Full `pnpm check:changed` needs authenticated Blacksmith Testbox or Crabbox coordinator access.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Branch autoreview passed clean after the current-main rebase and lint cleanup.

target.title: fix(cron): prune isolated-target cron sessions in session reaper
target.body:
## Summary

- `isCronRunSessionKey` only matches cron session keys containing `:run:` (`cron:jobId:run:runId`). Isolated-target cron jobs (`sessionTarget: isolated`) generate base session keys without `:run:` (`cron:jobId`), so the reaper never matched or pruned them. They accumulated indefinitely.
- **Why not broaden `isCronRunSessionKey`**: it is used in routing, session filtering, and heartbeat wake logic where it carries the specific semantic of "cron run attempt key". Broadening it would cause base cron sessions to be incorrectly remapped or hidden.
- Instead, add `isCronSessionKey` (which matches all `cron:`-prefixed keys) alongside `isCronRunSessionKey` in the reaper gate. The staleness check (`updatedAt < cutoff`) prevents deletion of active sessions.

## Verification

- All 17 session reaper tests pass
- All 71 session-key routing tests pass
- All 19 session-utils search tests pass
- 3 files changed, +5/-6 lines

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `cron.sessionRetention` has no effect on isolated-target cron jobs because the reaper only matches `:run:`-suffixed keys.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.22.0 on Linux, pnpm test.

**Steps run after the patch:**

1. Reaper tests:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts \
  src/cron/session-reaper.test.ts
```

2. Routing tests (failed in CI on first commit):
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts \
  src/routing/session-key.test.ts
```

3. Session search tests (failed in CI on first commit):
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts \
  src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
session-reaper.test.ts      Test Files  1 passed (1)  Tests  17 passed (17)
session-key.test.ts         Test Files  1 passed (1)  Tests  71 passed (71)
session-utils.search.ts     Test Files  1 passed (1)  Tests  19 passed (19)
```

The reaper gate at `session-reaper.ts:74`:

```text
Before:  if (!isCronRunSessionKey(key)) continue;
After:   if (!isCronRunSessionKey(key) && !isCronSessionKey(key)) continue;
```

| Function | Matches | Used in |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `isCronRunSessionKey` (unchanged) | `cron:jobId:run:runId` and descendants | routing, session filter, reaper |
| `isCronSessionKey` (new in reaper) | all `cron:`-prefixed keys | reaper only |

**Observed result:** Isolated cron base session keys are now pruned when stale. Fresh entries (within the retention window) are preserved by the `updatedAt < cutoff` check at line 82.

**Not tested:** Live gateway with isolated cron jobs observing session store pruning over 24h+.

Closes #89666.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid enumerating wrapped tool descriptors
target.body:
## Summary

- Stop before-tool-call and abort wrappers from spreading arbitrary active tool descriptors.
- Add a shared wrapper descriptor copier that preserves the `AgentTool` contract fields plus wrapper-relevant optional metadata without enumerating hostile plugin/tool fields.
- Cover descriptors with throwing non-contract getters and unreadable optional display metadata.

## Verification

- Red repro before fix:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.wrappers.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Failed with `non-contract tool field getter exploded` from `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook` and `wrapToolWithAbortSignal`.
- Focused local proof after fix:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.wrappers.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.wrappers.test.ts src/agents/tool-search.test.ts -t "agent tool wrappers|does not re-wrap abort-wrapped tools that already have before_tool_call hooks" --reporter=verbose`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts -t "fires hook exactly once when tool goes through wrap \\+ abort \\+ toToolDefinitions|preserves the hook marker when abort wrapping a hooked tool|normalizes outer code-mode exec hook params when a wrapper owns the hook" --reporter=verbose`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-tools.abort.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.wrapper-descriptor.ts src/agents/agent-tools.wrappers.test.ts`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/agent-tools.abort.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/agent-tools.wrapper-descriptor.ts src/agents/agent-tools.wrappers.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- Autoreview:
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Clean: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Remote/broad proof:
  - AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` run `run_1cdcddfdbd34`, lease `cbx_a7fdff5e57bd`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core, coreTests`.
  - That pre-amend run failed only in `typecheck core tests` on the new test's `"text"` literal inference; the test was fixed and local focused proof reran.
  - Rerunning AWS Crabbox on the amended head was blocked before lease creation by coordinator `401 unauthorized`; Blacksmith Testbox is also unavailable locally because `blacksmith testbox list --all` reports unauthenticated.
  - A local fallback for the exact failed core-test typecheck phase was blocked by sparse checkout inputs.
  - PR check rollup after creation: `mergeStateStatus=CLEAN`, active/failing checks 0.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A plugin/tool descriptor with a throwing extra getter can no longer crash assistant tool wrapping before content.

Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree with targeted Vitest wrapper and before-tool-call integration coverage; AWS Crabbox attempted for broad changed-gate proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.wrappers.test.ts src/agents/tool-search.test.ts -t "agent tool wrappers|does not re-wrap abort-wrapped tools that already have before_tool_call hooks" --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: 4 Vitest project files passed, 8 tests passed, 48 skipped; e2e wrapper composition target passed 3 tests with 16 skipped.

Observed result after fix: before-tool-call and abort wrappers keep the healthy tool descriptor and execution path while ignoring hostile non-contract fields and unreadable optional display metadata.

What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:changed` on the amended head under Crabbox/Testbox; the AWS rerun was blocked by coordinator auth, Blacksmith was unauthenticated, and local sparse checkout cannot run the changed-gate core-test typecheck. GitHub PR checks settled clean after push.

target.title: fix: suppress duplicate Telegram plugin approvals 🤖
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat a missing approval `turnSourceChannel` as unknown instead of a channel mismatch when checking native-delivery fallback suppression.
- This lets Telegram suppress the generic forwarding fallback when native delivery is enabled for the resolved approval account, avoiding duplicate plugin approval prompts.
- AI-assisted contribution; I reviewed the diff and targeted test results.

Fixes #75749

## Real behavior proof
- Reproduced the broken decision path at the shared delivery helper level: with `turnSourceChannel: null`, Telegram target channel, and native delivery enabled for the resolved account, the helper now returns `true` to suppress forwarding fallback.
- Verified by adding a regression to `src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.test.ts` that exercises the null-channel case and confirms the resolved account is still checked.
- I did not run a live Telegram bot/DM session in this environment.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(secrets): collect persona-level TTS provider SecretRefs
target.body:
Fixes #89607

## Summary

The secret collector walks `messages.tts.providers.*.apiKey` but does not walk `messages.tts.personas.*.providers.*.apiKey` or `agents.list[].tts.personas.*.providers.*.apiKey`. When a persona overrides the TTS provider config with a file-source SecretRef, the secret resolver never sees the ref, so the `apiKey` reaches the plugin as an unresolved object instead of the actual key string. The plugin then reports `not configured`.

This fix:
- Adds persona-level provider iteration in `collectTtsApiKeyAssignments`
- Registers two new target paths in the secret target registry (`messages.tts.personas.*.providers.*.apiKey` and `agents.list[].tts.personas.*.providers.*.apiKey`)
- Extends `hasAgentRuntimeSecretRefs` to detect persona-level TTS refs
- Adds 5 regression tests covering top-level, persona-level, combined, empty-persona, and agent-level persona paths

Closes #89607

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** File-source SecretRef in `messages.tts.personas.<id>.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey` is not resolved at runtime, causing `elevenlabs: not configured`.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/wt-89607`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd /tmp/wt-89607
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build

  # Verify the persona-level collector is in the production bundle
  cat dist/runtime-config-collectors-tts-BgBmCuWf.js

  # Run the regression tests
  npx vitest run src/secrets/runtime-config-collectors-tts.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  The persona-level iteration is active in the production bundle:

  ```console
  $ cat dist/runtime-config-collectors-tts-BgBmCuWf.js
  ...
  const personas = params.tts.personas;
  if (isRecord(personas)) for (const [personaId, persona] of Object.entries(personas)) {
  if (!isRecord(persona)) continue;
  const personaProviders = persona.providers;
  if (!isRecord(personaProviders)) continue;
  for (const [providerId, providerConfig] of Object.entries(personaProviders)) {
  if (!isRecord(providerConfig)) continue;
  collectProviderApiKeyAssignment({
  providerId,
  providerConfig,
  pathPrefix: `${params.pathPrefix}.personas.${personaId}`,
  ...
  ```

  Terminal confirms all 5 new tests exercise the persona SecretRef collection:

  ```console
  $ npx vitest run src/secrets/runtime-config-collectors-tts.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  ✓ collectTtsApiKeyAssignments > collects SecretRefs from top-level TTS providers
  ✓ collectTtsApiKeyAssignments > collects SecretRefs from persona-level TTS provider overrides
  ✓ collectTtsApiKeyAssignments > collects SecretRefs from both top-level and persona-level providers
  ✓ collectTtsApiKeyAssignments > skips personas without providers
  ✓ collectTtsApiKeyAssignments > collects persona refs with agent-level path prefix
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  5 passed (5)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Production bundle at `dist/runtime-config-collectors-tts-BgBmCuWf.js` iterates persona-level providers and calls `collectProviderApiKeyAssignment` for each, ensuring file-source SecretRefs at `messages.tts.personas.*.providers.*.apiKey` are resolved before reaching the TTS plugin.
- **What was not tested:** Live gateway with an ElevenLabs account and file-source secret provider, triggering `openclaw infer tts convert` to confirm audio synthesis succeeds with the resolved key.

target.title: fix(agents): wrap bundle MCP schema setup errors
target.body:
## Summary
- Wrap draft-2020-12 bundle MCP schema setup failures with the existing `Invalid MCP draft-2020-12 JSON Schema` error prefix.
- Preserve the original setup failure as `cause` and keep non-draft schemas on the SDK AJV validator path.
- Add a regression for a hostile MCP catalog schema whose `properties` proxy throws during schema format stripping.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- Blacksmith Testbox-through-Crabbox attempt: blocked by missing local auth (`not authenticated — run 'blacksmith auth login' first`).
- AWS Crabbox fallback: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (run `run_b5498eb1b264`, lease `cbx_e2ec73419b8c`, slug `jade-hermit`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, command 3m22.449s, total 5m46.496s; lease cleanup stopped=true).

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: hostile draft-2020-12 MCP catalog schemas can no longer leak raw setup-time traversal/compile failures past the bundle MCP schema validator.
Real environment tested: local Codex GWT on macOS using the repo Vitest wrapper and source lint/format wrappers; remote AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate for `pnpm check:changed`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.
Evidence after fix: the regression with a throwing `properties` proxy now throws `Invalid MCP draft-2020-12 JSON Schema: bundle schema properties ownKeys exploded`; remote changed gate selected `core, coreTests` and completed with exit 0.
Observed result after fix: focused Vitest passed 1 file / 31 tests; format, oxlint, diff check, local + branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed.
What was not tested: Blacksmith Testbox proof was not run because local Blacksmith auth is missing; AWS Crabbox supplied the remote changed-gate proof instead.

The PR threads a remaining startup deadline into Codex app-server initialize, stops connection-close retries after startup abandonment, and adds Codex startup plus isolated cron routing coverage.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +387. Total +419 across 5 files.

target.title: fix #84567: [Bug]: Codex bundled harness initialize still hangs in 2026.5.18 isolated cron — surfaces via #64744 timeout-wrapping as 'isolated agent setup timed out before runner start'
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

The root cause fixed here is a Codex app-server startup lifecycle timeout boundary: OpenClaw started the outer startup watchdog before Codex `initialize`, but the shared/isolated Codex client startup path did not receive the remaining startup deadline for that first required protocol request. When `client.initialize()` stalled after earlier startup work had consumed time, the broader startup watchdog could win first and surface a generic startup / isolated cron setup timeout instead of the blocked Codex initialize phase.

Why does this matter now?

For isolated cron runs routed through Codex, a stall before `thread/start` can currently look like `isolated agent setup timed out before runner start`, which hides the protocol phase that actually owns the failure once startup has reached `initialize`. The reporter's #84567 evidence proves the user-visible isolated cron setup timeout, `openai/gpt-5.5` / Codex routing, and a live idle Codex worker; it does not by itself prove the reporter-account run was already inside `client.initialize()`. The older #64744 evidence plus the deterministic proofs here cover the specific initialize lifecycle boundary.

What is the intended outcome?

Root-cause lifecycle fix for the Codex initialize timeout boundary: carry the startup deadline into the Codex app-server client factory, consume it only around `client.initialize()`, let the initialize timeout win before the outer watchdog, and stop connection-closed retries after the startup watchdog has already abandoned the attempt.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This does not change live OAuth auth selection, model routing, successful Codex app-server startup behavior, post-`thread/start` turn execution, user config, schema, protocol payloads, or public APIs. It also does not claim a full reporter-account live OpenAI OAuth recurring cron/provider turn.

What does success look like?

For runs that have reached Codex `initialize`, a stalled initialize request fails as `codex app-server initialize timed out` before `thread/start`, while pre-initialize factory/start-context hangs still report the generic startup timeout and successful initialize paths keep their existing behavior.

What should reviewers focus on?

Review the root invariant and risk boundary: `initialize` is the first required Codex protocol request before `thread/start`, so the remaining startup deadline must be enforced at that lifecycle owner instead of being left to a later generic startup watchdog. The timeout/retry change is intentionally narrow: only `client.initialize()` consumes the derived deadline, and connection-closed retries stop only after startup has already been abandoned.

- **Fix classification:** Root-cause Codex startup lifecycle and diagnostic-boundary fix for the `initialize` phase.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the pre-`thread/start` initialize stall boundary and the issue-shaped isolated cron runner route. Remaining external proof is a full reporter-account live OpenAI OAuth recurring cron/provider turn; this body states that boundary instead of claiming it.
- **Root cause:** OpenClaw's Codex startup owner created a single outer startup deadline, but the shared/isolated client startup owner did not receive that remaining deadline for the first Codex protocol request, `client.initialize()`. That mismatch let the generic startup watchdog own failures that had already reached the initialize lifecycle boundary.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch moves timeout ownership to the source lifecycle owner by passing an initialize deadline through the internal client factory options and consuming it only at `client.initialize()`. That fixes the root invariant that the first protocol request must complete or fail inside the existing startup budget, rather than masking the symptom after a generic cron setup timeout has already been reported.
- **Patch quality notes:** Timeout/retry changes are bounded: the initialize deadline is only consumed by the shared/isolated `client.initialize()` phase, callers that omit it keep the old factory-wide timeout behavior, pre-initialize factory hangs stay on the generic startup-timeout path, and retries stop only after the outer startup watchdog has abandoned startup.
- **What did NOT change:** OAuth profile selection, provider/model routing, successful Codex app-server startup, post-`thread/start` runtime behavior, public config, schema, protocol payloads, and public APIs.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is the Codex app-server lifecycle contract: `initialize` must complete before `thread/start`. OpenClaw now enforces the existing startup budget at that protocol lifecycle boundary instead of post-processing downstream cron setup errors.
- **Target test file:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts` and `src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression suite locks the stalled real-stdio initialize boundary, the pre-initialize generic startup-timeout boundary, abandoned-startup cleanup, connection-closed retry ownership after abandonment, and the real isolated cron `agentTurn` route for `openai/gpt-5.5` with the `openai-codex:default` profile id.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The production change is limited to internal startup deadline wiring and retry ownership; the new cron proof uses the real isolated cron runner plus a Codex app-server protocol harness to exercise the issue-shaped route deterministically without requiring reporter-only OAuth credentials.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

This PR is related to #84567 and #64744. It does not use an auto-closing keyword because #84567 still has a reporter-account live OAuth cron/provider proof gap.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #84567 and #64744. #84567 is the isolated cron setup-timeout report; #64744 provides older evidence that `initialize()` could remain pending without a bounded timeout.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request is claimed. This update responds to review feedback by tightening the root-cause/proof boundary and avoiding an overbroad reporter-account claim.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Stalled real stdio Codex initialize is bounded before `thread/start` and reports the initialize phase error.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, OpenClaw worktree after this patch, with a real stdio child process spawned through the Codex app-server client startup path.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):** copied output from the real-stdio proof:

```text
Behavior addressed: stalled real stdio Codex initialize is bounded before thread/start.
Real environment tested: Linux Node v22.22.0; spawned /usr/local/node/bin/node <evidence>/stalled-codex-app-server.mjs
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm exec tsx evidence/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts
Evidence after fix: observed error "codex app-server initialize timed out" after 9855 ms; onStartupTimeoutCalls=0; copied stub log lines are included below
Observed result after fix: initialize phase error won; generic codex app-server startup timed out was not surfaced.
What was not tested: a live OpenAI OAuth account or real Codex binary responding successfully after initialize.
```

Copied stub log from the real stdio child:

```text
2026-06-02T15:13:51.604Z stub-start pid=<redacted>
2026-06-02T15:13:51.609Z received method=initialize id=1
2026-06-02T15:14:00.757Z stub-exit signal=stdin-end
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The stdio child received the real `initialize` request, did not answer, OpenClaw raised `codex app-server initialize timed out`, and the generic `codex app-server startup timed out` message did not win.
- **What was not tested:** The stalled-stdio proof does not use a live OAuth account; it uses a deterministic real-stdio stalled app-server child to exercise the same pre-`thread/start` initialize boundary without external credentials.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** A full reporter-account live OpenAI OAuth recurring cron/provider turn is still not claimed. The reporter's #84567 live/idle worker evidence proves the user-visible setup-timeout route but does not by itself prove the exact stall was inside `client.initialize()`.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** #64744 reported that a manually driven child could answer JSON-RPC `initialize` quickly while OpenClaw's `CodexAppServerClient.initialize()` promise did not resolve; #84567 reported recurring isolated cron setup timeout with a live/idle Codex worker.

Additional isolated cron proof:

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The real isolated cron `agentTurn` runner path for `openai/gpt-5.5` selects the Codex harness, preserves the user-selected `openai-codex:default` auth profile id, attributes an initialize stall to the Codex app-server initialize phase, and reaches `thread/start` after a successful initialize response.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node/Vitest cron test runner, OpenClaw worktree after this patch, real `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn` execution path, and an in-memory Codex app-server protocol harness. The auth fixture uses a token-backed local credential for the `openai-codex:default` profile id with canonical provider `openai`; it does not claim a live reporter OAuth refresh.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts --testTimeout 60000 --reporter verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):** copied output from the isolated cron proof validation:

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 [local path redacted]

[vitest] still running with no output for 30000ms (run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts --testTimeout 60000 --reporter verbose --passWithNoTests=false).
 ✓ |cron| src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn Codex harness routing > attributes a Codex initialize stall from the selected harness to agent-run diagnostics 28123ms
 ✓ |cron| src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn Codex harness routing > uses the Codex harness for a successful isolated cron agentTurn 2167ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
   Start at  12:23:33
   Duration  50.55s (transform 30.96s, setup 1.09s, import 18.45s, tests 30.30s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The stalled-initialize cron test observed `initialize`, returned `codex app-server initialize timed out`, did not surface `isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` or `codex app-server startup timed out`, recorded the diagnostic under `agent-run` rather than `cron-setup`, and did not send `thread/start`. The successful-initialize cron test observed `thread/start`, responded with a schema-shaped Codex thread/start result, and the isolated cron run resolved with `status: "ok"`.
- **What was not tested:** This isolated cron harness does not use a live OAuth account or real Codex binary.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The deterministic cron proof covers runner/model/profile/thread-start routing and timeout attribution without consuming a live provider turn; it is not a full reporter-account recurring cron/provider execution.

Additional live Codex OAuth setup proof:

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A real managed Codex app-server using the local `openai-codex:default` OAuth profile reaches `initialize` and `thread/start` before any agent/model turn starts.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS Darwin 25.5.0 arm64, Node `v24.15.0`, OpenClaw worktree `2c374aa98921`, Codex app-server `0.135.0`, managed `codex app-server --listen stdio://`, temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, auth profile copied into a temporary agent SQLite auth DB with token values redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
PROOF_REPO='<worktree>' \
PROOF_AUTH_PROFILES_PATH='<local auth-profiles.json>' \
PROOF_AUTH_PROFILE_ID='openai-codex:default' \
PROOF_MODEL='gpt-5.5' \
  env -C '<worktree>' \
  node --import tsx '<evidence>/live-codex-oauth-setup-proof.mjs' \
  > '<evidence>/live-codex-oauth-setup-proof.out' \
  2> '<evidence>/live-codex-oauth-setup-proof.err'
```

- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):** copied redacted output from the live setup proof:

```json
{
  "behavior": "live Codex app-server OAuth setup reaches initialize and thread/start before any agent turn",
  "platform": "Darwin 25.5.0",
  "arch": "arm64",
  "node": "v24.15.0",
  "head": "2c374aa98921",
  "profileId": "openai-codex:default",
  "model": "openai/gpt-5.5",
  "authDatabasePath": "state/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite",
  "profileBefore": {
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "type": "oauth",
    "hasAccess": true,
    "hasRefresh": true,
    "expiredAtStart": true,
    "keyNames": ["access", "accountId", "expires", "managedBy", "provider", "refresh", "type"]
  },
  "profileAfter": {
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "type": "oauth",
    "hasAccess": true,
    "hasRefresh": true,
    "expiredAtStart": true,
    "keyNames": ["access", "accountId", "expires", "provider", "refresh", "type"]
  },
  "appServerStart": {
    "transport": "stdio",
    "command": "codex",
    "commandSource": "managed",
    "args": ["app-server", "--listen", "stdio://"]
  },
  "initialized": true,
  "serverVersion": "0.135.0",
  "initializeDurationMs": 3366,
  "threadStartDurationMs": 1137,
  "threadStart": {
    "model": "gpt-5.5",
    "modelProvider": "openai",
    "thread": {
      "id": "<redacted>",
      "statusType": "idle",
      "ephemeral": false,
      "cwd": "<temp workspace>"
    }
  },
  "noModelTurnStarted": true,
  "elapsedMs": 4503
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The managed Codex app-server completed `initialize`, accepted the OAuth-backed setup, and returned an idle `thread/start` response for `openai/gpt-5.5` with `modelProvider: "openai"`; no generic setup timeout occurred and no model turn was started.
- **What was not tested:** This live proof stops before sending a provider/model prompt and does not run the recurring cron scheduler on the reporter account.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The deterministic isolated cron proof covers the cron runner route and timeout attribution without consuming a live provider turn; maintainers may still request a full reporter-account live OAuth cron/provider turn before treating all of #84567 as fully root-caused.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 10 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "times out app-server startup before thread setup can hang forever"`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 1 test passed / 104 skipped.
- `pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.codex-app-server.test.ts --testTimeout 60000 --reporter verbose`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 2 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/client-factory.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
  - Result: no lint findings.
- `pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts`
  - Result: copied in **Real behavior proof** above.
- `node --import tsx <evidence>/live-codex-oauth-setup-proof.mjs`
  - Result: managed Codex app-server completed `initialize`, returned idle `thread/start`, and no model turn was started.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added/updated regression coverage for stalled real-stdio initialize timeout attribution, remaining startup budget calculation, pre-initialize factory timeout boundary, startup-timeout cleanup, cancellation cleanup, startup RPC timeout behavior, connection-closed retry ownership after abandonment, and the isolated cron `agentTurn` route for `openai/gpt-5.5` via the Codex harness.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before this fix, a stalled initialize after earlier startup work could let the broader startup watchdog surface a generic app-server startup / isolated cron setup timeout before the Codex initialize lifecycle owner reported `codex app-server initialize timed out`.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added and updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. For Codex startup attempts that reach `initialize` and then stall, the user-visible diagnostic changes from a generic startup/setup timeout to `codex app-server initialize timed out`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Availability during Codex app-server startup, because timeout ordering and connection-closed retry ownership changed around the first protocol request.

How is that risk mitigated?

The root-cause mitigation is scoped to the existing startup budget: startup passes an initialize deadline with a 150ms head start so the shared/isolated `client.initialize()` timeout can win before the outer watchdog. The optional internal factory field is only set by the Codex attempt startup path; callers that omit it keep previous timeout behavior. Pre-initialize factory/start-context hangs still use the generic startup timeout path, and connection-closed retries stop only after the startup watchdog has already abandoned the attempt. Focused tests and ClawSweeper review both covered this boundary.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review after this proof-boundary update; remote checks should run on the pushed head.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

No known local author action remains after this update. Maintainers still need to decide whether the deterministic local isolated cron proof plus the real-stdio stalled initialize proof plus the live local OAuth Codex app-server setup proof are sufficient lifecycle/root-cause boundary evidence, or whether a full reporter-account live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron/provider turn is required before treating #84567 as fully root-caused. This update does not claim a model turn or recurring cron fire on the reporter account, and it does not claim that the reporter's live/idle worker snapshot alone proves the hang was inside `client.initialize()`.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- **RF-001:** Maintainer-decision needed. This update keeps the remaining live OAuth cron/provider-turn limitation disclosed and adds live local OAuth Codex app-server setup proof, so maintainers can decide whether the remaining full reporter-account cron gap is acceptable.
- **RF-002:** Fixed/disclosed. The body explains the intentional timeout/retry ordering change and the risk boundary: stalled `initialize` now fails before the outer startup watchdog, and connection-closed retries stop only after the watchdog has abandoned startup.
- **RF-003:** Fixed as far as local proof allows; external proof unavailable. This update adds the real isolated cron runner path for `agentTurn`, `openai/gpt-5.5`, and the `openai-codex:default` profile id, plus live local OAuth Codex app-server setup through `thread/start`; it still does not claim a full reporter-account recurring cron/provider turn or direct reporter-environment proof that #84567's live/idle worker was stalled inside `client.initialize()`.
- **RF-004:** Disclosed. The remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of this Codex startup lifecycle/root-cause proof boundary, not another narrow automated code repair.

ClawSweeper commit review result: `nothing_found`, high confidence, no findings. One sandbox-only WebSocketServer test limitation was reported as unrelated to this change path.

target.title: fix(trajectory): isolate manifest metadata rows
target.body:
## Summary

- isolate unreadable manifest snapshot rows while building trajectory support metadata
- preserve sorted plugin support metadata for healthy rows
- add a trajectory metadata regression with a poisoned manifest row before a healthy plugin row

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts` failed pre-fix with `trajectory manifest id exploded`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/trajectory/metadata.ts src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/trajectory/metadata.ts src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` failed before sync/lease because the Crabbox sparse-sync root had `746.0 MiB` free and requires `1.00 GiB`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: trajectory run metadata capture now skips unreadable plugin manifest rows instead of aborting support metadata capture for the whole run.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw Codex worktree on branch `fuzz-tool-schema-next137-20260603`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/trajectory/metadata.ts src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/trajectory/metadata.ts src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 1 file / 7 tests; formatting and lint checks passed; diff whitespace check passed; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings and `overall: patch is correct (0.84)`.

Observed result after fix: a manifest snapshot with a poisoned `id` getter still records the later healthy plugin in `metadata.plugins`.

What was not tested: full `pnpm check:changed`; the required Crabbox/Testbox broad gate could not start because local disk was below the Crabbox sparse-sync preflight minimum.

target.title: fix: keep inbound metadata in runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the remaining #72704-shaped path where current-turn inbound channel metadata was still prepended into the user prompt text.

Current `main` already builds ordinary inbound metadata as `currentInboundContext`, but `run/attempt.ts` immediately merged that context back into both `promptForSession` and `promptForModel` via `buildCurrentInboundPrompt(...)`. That kept Telegram `Conversation info` / `Sender` blocks on the model-visible user prompt path and could still persist the metadata-heavy prompt shape into active session state.

This PR moves `currentInboundContext` into the existing runtime-context channel instead:

- user prompt text stays user-authored (`promptForSession` / `promptForModel` are no longer prefixed with current inbound metadata)
- current-turn metadata is still available to the model as hidden prompt-local runtime context before the active user turn
- extracted hidden runtime context and current inbound context are combined in one runtime-context payload
- transcript/user prompt text remains clean

## Linked Issue

Closes #72704

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram/channel current-turn metadata (`Conversation info` / `Sender`) was still being prepended into the user prompt path after it had been assembled as `currentInboundContext`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on Linux, running the real TypeScript runtime module through `node --import tsx` (no mocks, no test runner).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'const { resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts, buildRuntimeContextCustomMessage } = await import("./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts"); const parts = resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts({ effectivePrompt: "Please execute your workflow", transcriptPrompt: "Please execute your workflow", currentInboundContext: { text: ["Conversation info (untrusted metadata):", "```json", JSON.stringify({ channel: "telegram", message_id: "14636" }), "```", "", "Sender (untrusted metadata):", "```json", JSON.stringify({ id: "redacted", name: "Sherina" }), "```"].join("\n") } }); const runtimeMessage = buildRuntimeContextCustomMessage(parts.runtimeContext); console.log(JSON.stringify({ prompt: parts.prompt, modelPrompt: parts.modelPrompt ?? null, promptContainsMetadata: parts.prompt.includes("Conversation info") || parts.prompt.includes("Sender (untrusted metadata)"), runtimeContextContainsMetadata: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes("Conversation info (untrusted metadata):") && parts.runtimeContext?.includes("Sender (untrusted metadata):")), runtimeMessageCustomType: runtimeMessage?.customType, runtimeMessageDisplay: runtimeMessage?.display }, null, 2));'
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "prompt": "Please execute your workflow",
  "modelPrompt": null,
  "promptContainsMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsMetadata": true,
  "runtimeMessageCustomType": "openclaw.runtime-context",
  "runtimeMessageDisplay": false
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the active user prompt remains exactly the user-authored text, metadata is not present in `prompt`/`modelPrompt`, and the same metadata is preserved in a non-displayed `openclaw.runtime-context` custom message.
- What was not tested: a full live Telegram delivery round-trip with an actual bot account and model call.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a local runtime-boundary proof rather than a provider-level screenshot; it exercises the real OpenClaw prompt/runtime-context code path without mocks.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): on current `main`, `run/attempt.ts` called `buildCurrentInboundPrompt({ context: params.currentInboundContext, ... })` for both `promptForSession` and `promptForModel`, which prepended the metadata context into the prompt text.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts` - 78 files / 1299 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts` - 35 tests passed across 2 shards
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` - passed

Regression coverage added:

- Telegram-style `Conversation info` / `Sender` metadata remains out of `parts.prompt`
- that metadata remains available in `parts.runtimeContext`
- current inbound metadata combines with extracted hidden runtime context instead of replacing it
- embedded-agent context-engine turns preserve reply/event context through `openclaw.runtime-context` without prepending it to the active prompt

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a guarded Gateway helper for `plugins.uiDescriptors` plus a contract test so an unreadable Control UI descriptor registration is skipped while healthy descriptors still return.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +65. Total +93 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main dereferences `entry.descriptor` inside one unguarded `plugins.uiDescriptors` projection, so a throwing getter can abort the handler before healthy rows return. I did not run the test because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft is conflicting and has a same-root-cause sibling, so maintainers need to choose and rebase one landing branch rather than queue automated repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): guard plugin UI descriptors
target.body:
## Summary

- guard `plugins.uiDescriptors` descriptor projection so one malformed Control UI registration cannot crash the whole gateway response
- skip unreadable or malformed UI descriptor rows while preserving healthy plugin descriptors
- add a contract regression for an unreadable descriptor getter next to a healthy descriptor

## Verification

Behavior addressed: A plugin registration with an unreadable `descriptor` getter could throw inside `plugins.uiDescriptors`, preventing healthy plugin UI descriptors from being returned.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw linked Codex worktree on Node 26 with the rebased branch `fuzz-tool-schema-next91-20260603` at `21e5ba12fff`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-next91-rebase-focused node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts -t "keeps returning healthy UI descriptors after unreadable registrations" --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --maxConcurrency=1`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-next91-rebase-full node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --maxConcurrency=1`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 1 test; full contract file passed 1 file / 11 tests. Format, lint, and diff whitespace checks passed. Branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.88)`.

Observed result after fix: The gateway returns the healthy `healthy-ui` descriptor and skips the unreadable `broken-ui` registration instead of throwing `control UI descriptor getter exploded`.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` could not be run through maintainer remote gates from this shell: `blacksmith testbox list --all` reported unauthenticated, and AWS Crabbox failed before lease creation with coordinator `401 unauthorized`. `agent-transcript find` returned `[]`.

commands.review_summary:
The PR changes gateway `send` to reuse the resolved runtime config snapshot when it matches the caller source config, and adds gateway/Feishu tests for SecretRef-backed credentials reaching media delivery.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +101. Total +102 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main documents Feishu appSecret as a SecretRef surface, the strict Feishu runtime resolver throws on non-env SecretRefs, and media/outbound callers can still pass unresolved cfg into that path. I did not run a live Feishu tenant send in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This external draft needs contributor proof and maintainer selection among overlapping Feishu fix candidates rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(feishu): use resolved runtime config for gateway sends
target.body:
## Summary
- use the hot runtime config snapshot for gateway send requests when it matches the caller source config, so resolved Feishu SecretRef credentials reach delivery
- keep Feishu mediaUrl as a plain payload input instead of resolving media URLs through SecretRef
- add regression coverage for the gateway send handoff and Feishu media upload client credentials

Fixes #89338.

## Validation
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts: passed (gateway 57 tests, Feishu 126 tests)
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
- git diff --check

## Remaining
- redacted live Feishu proof is still needed before this draft should be marked ready
